data.description:
Rec 5 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (risk R7). _native.py inserts the engine dir into sys.path so ticket_reducer, ticket_graph, ticket_resolver, rebar_reconciler import as TOP-LEVEL module names; engine_env() does the same via PYTHONPATH for subprocesses. Generic top-level names risk host collisions, defeat static tooling, and extend the unpacked-install constraint beyond bash.

Work:
- ticket_reducer -> rebar.reducer; ticket_graph -> rebar.graph; rebar_reconciler -> rebar.reconciler; ticket_txn/ticket_output/ticket_resolver -> rebar._engine_support (or fold into a future rebar._commands).
- Delete the sys.path insertion in _native.py; restrict engine_env()'s PYTHONPATH hack to bash-only callers.
- Keep thin top-level shims (re-export modules) ONLY while bash heredocs still import the old names; delete with the strangler-fig Tier A/B ports.
- Exclude __pycache__ from the wheel; verify pyproject artifacts glob output (src/rebar/_engine/__pycache__/*.pyc currently at risk of shipping).

BREAKING-WINDOW: import-path rename is free today (zero external users); in six months it is a deprecation program. Public library re-exports (rebar.reduce_ticket etc.) do not change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No engine package importable as a top-level generic name from the library path
- [ ] Wheel contains no __pycache__/.pyc; contents verified
- [ ] Full suite green; reconciler sys.modules identity keys handled deliberately (rename or shim, documented)
data.title: Repackage engine Python as rebar.* subpackages (kill sys.path injection)
